qa_pairs.question: What is the purpose of the Advanced Research Computing infrastructure renewal, and what benefits does it offer Canadian researchers?
qa_pairs.answer: The Advanced Research Computing infrastructure renewal began in winter 2024–2025 to improve High Performance Computing (HPC) and Cloud services for Canadian researchers. This initiative replaced nearly 80% of equipment approaching end-of-life, resulting in faster processing speeds, greater storage capacity, and improved reliability.

qa_pairs.question: Which new systems are replacing the older Advanced Research Computing clusters as part of the infrastructure renewal?
qa_pairs.answer: As part of the infrastructure renewal, Rorqual replaced Béluga, Fir replaced Cedar, Trillium replaced Niagara and Mist, and Nibi replaced Graham. The Arbutus Cloud also underwent an upgrade, though its interface remained unchanged.

qa_pairs.question: What actions are required for users with existing allocations on the legacy Arbutus system during its upgrade and migration period?
qa_pairs.answer: Both RAC recipients and RAS users with a 2025–2026 allocation on legacy Arbutus are responsible for migrating their existing resources before August 31, 2026. Migration can commence as soon as access to the new Arbutus Cloud is available. Additionally, projects with existing Arbutus RAS Cloud allocations must submit a request to cloud@tech.alliancecan.ca to be added to the provisioning queue.
